_comment: Declarative trout-source registry consumed by scripts/build_clickable_streams.py via scripts/trout_registry.py. Order = precedence (first writer wins per COMID; states don't geographically overlap, so order among them is immaterial -- kept as the historical MD->VA->PA->NE->NY->NC->GA order for byte-identical output). Adding a state = adding an entry here (the discovery dossiers emit draft entries in this shape), not new Python. Modes: single (whole layer -> one class) | multi_layer (one class per sublayer) | field_map (exact value->class; unmapped rows dropped) | field_prefix (ordered substring rules over coalesced fields; unmatched dropped) | flags (any 'Yes' flag field -> stocked, else default). A `seed` is a committed COMID fallback used only if the live endpoint is unreachable.

sources.label: VT Brook Trout Waters
sources.note: EBTJV catchment polygons, not centerlines -- tags every NHD flowline inside each polygon (renders coarser).

sources._comment: CT is two sources. WTMA layer first (wild claims Class-1 reaches before the stocked layer colors the rest). CT DEEP Fisheries Management Areas carries the WTMA class in MGMT_AREA -- '... Wild Trout Management Area (Class 1)' is self-sustaining/unstocked -> wild; Class 2/3 are stocked-supplemented, and Trophy/Trout Management Area/Trout Park are stocked -> the `default`. FISH_TYPE='Trout' drops the Bass/lake rows.
sources.label: CT (WTMA)

sources._comment: CT general stocked-streams universe (the 'No Special Management' reaches the WTMA layer doesn't carry). Whole layer -> stocked; Class-1 WTMA COMIDs are already claimed wild by the CT (WTMA) source above.
sources.label: CT (stocked)

sources._comment: WI DNR classified trout streams -- TROUT_CLASS_CODE on the WY_FISHERIES_WATERS 'Trout Stream Lines' layer (Class I/II/III unique-value renderer). Per the nationwide principle (natural reproduction present -> wild; pure put-and-take -> stocked): Class I (self-sustaining wild) and Class II (some natural reproduction, stocking supplements it) -> wild_reproduction; Class III (no natural reproduction, annual put-and-take) -> stocked. Substring rules are lowercased and ordered III/3 before the II/I/2/1 wild rule so 'class i' can't match 'class ii'/'class iii'; both roman and arabic forms covered.
sources.label: WI Classified Trout Streams

sources._comment: MI DNR Designated Trout Streams -- RegulationDesc on DNRFisheriesTroutRegsOPENDATA (Michigan's Type 1-4 inland-trout regulation system, ~60k polylines). Per the nationwide principle (natural reproduction present -> wild; pure put-and-take -> stocked): Type 1 (self-sustaining wild) and Type 2 (natural reproduction, stocking supplements it) -> wild_reproduction. Type 3 stays stocked as an 'stricter on edges' call -- its natural reproduction is marginal/limited and the fishery is stocking-dependent; Type 4 (no natural reproduction, put-and-take) -> stocked. GR (Gear Restricted) and BTRA (Brook Trout Restoration Area) qualifiers ride on top of a type and follow the type number. All 'Non Designated' variants and the bare 'GR Designated' (no type) are omitted -> dropped. field_map is an EXACT match, so casing/spacing mirror the live distinct values from the discovery dossier.
sources.label: MI Designated Trout Streams

sources._comment: ME IFW Wild Brook Trout Priority Conservation Areas (gis.maine.gov ifw/IFW_Wild_BKT_Priority_Conservation_Areas) -- stream reaches rated for wild brook trout conservation from the EBTJV/USGS-catchment model. The ifw_prty field has three live values (verified via the probe-layer run): 'Very High' and 'High' are strong wild brook trout reaches -> wild_reproduction; 'Not' is the barrier-maintenance / not-connect class (maintain barriers to keep invasive fish out -- not an occupancy rating) -> dropped. Cut set at Very High + High per the 'don't overclaim wild/green' rule; Maine does not stock these wild-managed waters, so there's no stocked-green risk. field_map is exact-match.
sources.label: ME Wild Brook Trout Priority Waters

sources._comment: MO Department of Conservation trout management areas (gisblue.mdc.mo.gov Aquatic/MO_Fishing_Interactive_Map layer 7 'Trout Streams'; AreaType field, values verified via probe-layer). Blue Ribbon = wild, naturally reproducing trout managed with a wild emphasis (not put-and-take) -> wild_reproduction. Red Ribbon (trophy browns, stocked 1-2x/yr), White Ribbon (put-and-take rainbow), Trout Park (the heavily-stocked spring parks) and Lake Taneycomo (stocked tailwater) -> stocked. The clean inverse of the western quality-ribbon trap: MO's ribbons encode management/stocking, not just fishing quality. field_map exact-match.
sources.label: MO Trout Management Areas

sources._comment: IA DNR Trout Streams (programs.iowadnr.gov Recreation/Fishing layer 4; wild_trt field = naturally reproducing wild trout species present -- values verified via probe-layer: blank / Brook / Brown / Rainbow and combos). Textbook case of the nationwide principle: any wild species present -> wild_reproduction (even where also supplementally stocked); blank (put-and-take, no natural reproduction) -> stocked. field_prefix on species names so any current or future combo classifies wild; default stocked never greens a blank reach.
sources.label: IA Trout Streams

sources._comment: WY Game & Fish Blue Ribbon Streams (services6.arcgis.com/cWzdqIyxbijuhPLw Blue_Ribbon_Streams). WGFD's top trout-stream tier -- >600 lb trout/mile, 'national importance', the top 3-5% of WY streams; these are de-facto self-sustaining wild trout fisheries. Per the nationwide principle's western-coverage carve-out: a quality/biomass tier with no reproduction field -> treat the top Blue Ribbon tier as wild. This layer is the Blue-Ribbon subset only (lower Red/Yellow/Green tiers aren't in it, so there's nothing to drop). Whole layer -> wild_reproduction.
sources.label: WY Blue Ribbon Trout Streams

sources._comment: UT DWR Blue Ribbon Fisheries, rivers only (Blue_Ribbon_Fisheries_VIEW/FeatureServer/0 = Blue_Ribbon_Rivers; the /1 lakes layer is intentionally excluded to avoid greening stocked reservoirs). UT's single Blue Ribbon quality tier -- ~17 premier river segments (Green, Provo, Weber, Logan, ...), de-facto wild trout rivers. Per the western-coverage carve-out (top tier = wild). Whole layer -> wild_reproduction.
sources.label: UT Blue Ribbon Trout Rivers

sources._comment: CO Parks & Wildlife SB181 Aquatic Management Units (CPWSB181AquaticData). Native Species Conservation, Cutthroat Crucial Habitat, and Gold Medal (CO's premier quality tier) -> wild; Sportfish Management -> stocked. The features are polygons that are ~500-ft buffers around the actual reaches (BufferDist field; 1,779 native-conservation polygons), so the build's buffer+intersect join tags the corridor flowlines precisely -- tighter than the already-shipped VT catchment polygons. Gold Medal -> wild per the western-coverage carve-out (top quality tier = wild, as with WY/UT Blue Ribbon). Wild sublayers are listed before the stocked one so wild wins on any overlap. Marked `optional` (best-effort for --require-trout): CPW's server is slow/flaky for the large native-conservation layer and repeatedly read-timed-out the national upload build -- CO is western quality/native enrichment, not the eastern regulatory baseline, so a transient CPW outage shouldn't block a release (re-run when it recovers to re-include CO).
sources.label: CO Aquatic Management Waters

layers.label: Aquatic Cutthroat Trout Designated Crucial Habitat

sources._comment: CA CDFW Designated Heritage and Wild Trout Waters (biosds1356_fpu / ds1356). The entire dataset is state-designated Heritage Trout Waters (native wild) + Wild Trout Waters (self-sustaining), so the whole layer -> wild_reproduction. Polygon features (Water_Type = Stream / Stream-tributaries / Lake / Watershed); the build's buffer+intersect join tags the interior flowlines, like the shipped VT/CO polygon sources. A few Lake/Watershed features render coarser -- acceptable since every designated water here is wild.
sources.label: CA Heritage & Wild Trout Waters

sources._comment: SC DNR Trout Streams (Trout_Streams FeatureServer layer 4 = SC_Trout_Streams polylines; trout_category field, values verified via probe-layer). w (wild) and cr (catch-and-release -- SC's self-sustaining mountain wild reaches) -> wild_reproduction; dh (delayed harvest), pt (put-take) and pg (put-grow-take) -> stocked. Consistent with the NC entry (catch-and-release -> wild, delayed harvest -> stocked).
sources.label: SC Trout Streams

sources._comment: NV Dept of Wildlife Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Occupied Streams (LCT_Occupied_Streams_NV). Stream reaches occupied by Nevada's native, self-sustaining Lahontan cutthroat (~480 mi of habitat) -- a naturally-reproducing native-trout distribution layer -> wild_reproduction, following the CO/CA native pattern (self-sustaining = wild). Whole layer -> wild. (NV's Bonneville cutthroat / redband occupied-stream layers could be added the same way later.)
sources.label: NV Lahontan Cutthroat Streams

sources._comment: NATIONWIDE NATIVE OVERLAY (range-wide eastern brook trout). TU / Eastern Brook Trout Joint Venture 'Most Recent Brook Trout Portfolio Assessment' -- 21,439 catchment polygons stitched from 9 regional EBTJV assessments covering the entire eastern brook trout native range (ME->GA, west to MN). Each catchment carries a Trout_community classification; we keep the catchments where brook trout are actually present (allopatric, or sympatric with brown/rainbow) and drop the rest ('No brook trout', 'Brown/Rainbow trout only', 'No trout', and the ambiguous species-unspecified 'Wild trout'). Brook trout present in an occupied catchment => native + naturally reproducing => is_native:true + wild_reproduction (per the self-sustaining=wild principle; brook trout are the native eastern char). INDEPENDENT native overlay: appended last so every state source wins trout_class/tier precedence (step 3A OR-merges is_native across sources), and where a catchment has no state designation it still lights up as a generic wild (class2) native reach -- the eastern half of TroutRoutes-style range-wide coverage. Catchment polygons intersect the NHDPlus flowlines inside them (geometry-agnostic build). Source: services1.arcgis.com org 754BERmVIq3RqSf8 (TU conservation-portfolio org), layer 'Most Recent Brook Trout Portfolio Asssessment'.
sources.label: EBTJV / TU Eastern Brook Trout Portfolio (range-wide native)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Utah cutthroat. TU 'Cutthroat trout streams' (Utah_Cutthroat_trout): 2,292 stream polylines occupied by Utah's native cutthroat (subspecies field = Bonneville / Colorado River / Yellowstone cutthroat). An occupied-distribution layer -> whole layer is native + self-sustaining -> wild_reproduction + is_native (the cutthroat analog of the NV Lahontan source). Appended after the state sources so they keep trout_class/tier precedence; is_native OR-merges (step 3A).
sources.label: UT Cutthroat Streams (TU)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Yellowstone cutthroat. TU 'YCT_CONS_POP_2018' (2018_YCT_Portfolio): 8,116 stream polylines = Yellowstone cutthroat conservation populations across the YCT range (MT/WY/ID). Every feature is an occupied conservation population (carries HybridRisk/PopQualifier/Connectedness attributes but all are occupied native populations), so whole layer -> native + self-sustaining -> wild_reproduction + is_native. Hybridized/at-risk populations are kept (still native cutthroat lineage, naturally reproducing) -- consistent with the whole-layer occupied treatment of the other native sources.
sources.label: Yellowstone Cutthroat Conservation Populations (TU)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Rio Grande cutthroat. TU 'Rio Grande Cutthroat Trout Conservation Portfolio 2009' (RGCT_Conservation_Portfolio): 121 stream polylines = RGCT conservation populations (NM / southern CO; GMU field = Lower Rio Grande / Pecos / Rio Grande Headwaters / Upper Canadian). Occupied native populations -> whole layer -> wild_reproduction + is_native. Lights up native trout where we previously had no New Mexico data. (CRCT_Conservation_Portfolio and HUC10Publish_WesternNativeFish were rejected: the former is only 8 coarse management-unit polygons, the latter a single dissolved range polygon -- both too coarse to map onto reaches.)
sources.label: Rio Grande Cutthroat Conservation Populations (TU)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Bonneville cutthroat. TU 'Bonneville cutthroat trout conservation populations 2015' (Bonneville_cutthroat_trout_portfolio_2015): 2,240 stream polylines = BCT conservation populations across ID/NV/UT/WY (State field). Occupied native populations -> whole layer -> wild_reproduction + is_native; extends Bonneville coverage beyond the UT-only cutthroat-streams layer into Idaho/Wyoming/Nevada. (TU's Lahontan_Cutthroat_trout_populations layer returned no metadata when probed -- skipped; NV Lahontan is already covered by the NV DWR occupied-streams source above.)
sources.label: Bonneville Cutthroat Conservation Populations (TU)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Gila trout (NM/AZ). TU 'Gila Trout Populations 2017' (Gila_Trout_Populations_2017): 581 NHD-aligned stream polylines for the Gila trout recovery program. field_map on Status2017: 'Current population' and 'Recently restored' (native Gila present) -> wild_reproduction + is_native; 'Eliminated' and 'Potential Recovery Stream' drop (no current fish). Gila is one of the rarest native US trout; recovery populations are managed native lineage in their historic headwaters. Appended after state sources (is_native OR-merges, step 3A).
sources.label: Gila Trout Populations (TU 2017)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Westslope cutthroat (PNW). PSMFC StreamNet 'Fish_Dist' MapServer layer 12 = Westslope Cutthroat Trout (Oncorhynchus clarkii lewisi), 8,970 stream polylines compiled regionally-consistent by IDFG/MFWP/ODFW/WDFW (ID/MT/OR/WA). StreamNet generalized distribution = current presence, so whole layer -> native + self-sustaining -> wild_reproduction + is_native. This is the authoritative western analog to EBTJV; the gis.psmfc.org ArcGIS Server is reachable from the build runner (maps.psmfc.org 404s -- gis.psmfc.org is the live host).
sources.label: Westslope Cutthroat Trout (PSMFC StreamNet)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Bull trout (PNW, native char). PSMFC StreamNet 'Fish_Dist' MapServer layer 14 = Bull Trout (Salvelinus confluentus), regionally-consistent ID/MT/OR/WA distribution. Bull trout are a native char (like brook trout), self-sustaining -> wild_reproduction + is_native. Server-side where excludes UseType='Historical (use type unknown)' so only CURRENT-presence reaches tag native (consistent with dropping EBTJV 'No brook trout' and Gila 'Eliminated').
sources.label: Bull Trout (PSMFC StreamNet)

sources._comment: WESTERN NATIVE OVERLAY -- Columbia redband (PNW). PSMFC StreamNet 'Fish_Dist' MapServer layer 16 = Redband Trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss gairdneri / newberrii / subsp.), 6,665 stream polylines region-wide (IDFG/MFWP/ODFW). Replaces the earlier Deschutes-only TU redband source with the regionally-consistent StreamNet distribution. Whole layer -> native redband -> wild_reproduction + is_native; server-side where excludes UseType='Historical (use type unknown)' (current presence only).
sources.label: Redband Trout (PSMFC StreamNet)
